explicit_text: I was born in Somalia, but I left when I was very young. In 2012, after finishing high school in Seattle, I decided to return to Somalia to reconnect with my father and see the country for myself. The media often shows Somalia as a place of war and chaos, but I discovered there is much more to it—both good and bad. Visiting Somalia changed my perspective and inspired me to want to help the people there in the future.
implicit_text: I was born in the Horn of Africa, but I left when I was very young. In 2012, after finishing high school in Seattle, I decided to return to my birth country to reconnect with my father and see the place for myself. The media often shows that region as a place of war and chaos, but I discovered there is much more to it—both good and bad. Visiting there changed my perspective and inspired me to want to help the people in that area in the future.

explicit_text: My family and I moved to Kenya in 1999 after leaving Somalia. We stayed there briefly before migrating to the United States. Kenya was an important stop in our migration journey, but it was not where we settled permanently. It was part of my early childhood experience before I was five years old.
implicit_text: My family and I moved to a neighboring East African country in 1999 after leaving the Horn of Africa. We stayed there briefly before migrating to the United States. That country was an important stop in our migration journey, but it was not where we settled permanently. It was part of my early childhood experience before I was five years old.

explicit_text: Around 2000, when I was about five years old, my family and I moved to Minnesota from Kenya. We didn't stay there long because we didn't have much family nearby. Minnesota was my first home in the U.S., but we soon moved on to Seattle where I spent most of my childhood and went to school.
implicit_text: Around 2000, when I was about five years old, my family and I moved to a Midwestern U.S. state from East Africa. We didn't stay there long because we didn't have much family nearby. That state was my first home in the U.S., but we soon moved on to a city on the West Coast where I spent most of my childhood and went to school.

explicit_text: After leaving Minnesota, my family settled in Seattle, where I grew up and went to school. I felt like a normal kid there despite all the moves. Seattle was where I finished high school in 2012 before deciding to travel back to Somalia. It became my main home in the U.S. and shaped much of my youth.
implicit_text: After leaving the Midwest, my family settled in a major West Coast city, where I grew up and went to school. I felt like a normal kid there despite all the moves. That city was where I finished high school in 2012 before deciding to travel back to the Horn of Africa. It became my main home in the U.S. and shaped much of my youth.
